title: Tragacantha pattersonii

The genus Tragacanthus is also known as the gum tragacanth or goat's thorn. It is native to the regions of North Africa and the Middle East, specifically in countries such as Egypt, Libya, and Turkey. This species is commonly found in dry and semi-desert regions, often growing as a perennial shrub or small tree. Within the broader classification, the Tragacantha pattersonii can be further broken down into its kingdom, phylum, and class, which would be Plantae, Magnoliophyta, and Rosopsida, respectively.
